name: Production Static SEO Assets And Merchant Feed Deployment Parity
decision: R-3 local source, build output, and local preview static asset parity are complete. Live www deployment still fails static SEO asset parity and must be fixed at the hosting/deployment layer before release.

implemented.summary: Adds a deterministic R-3 gate for source/build static SEO assets and optional live host parity.

removed.summary: Removed generated macOS metadata from the public deployment source directory.

localStaticAssets.finding: Local source and build output contain the required static SEO assets and Merchant feed. Build output is the correct deployment artifact directory for this repo.

providerReadback.finding: Provider readback and hosting writes are unavailable from the current project wrapper state.

ownerActions.action: Deploy the current build output directory build to the production www host.
Ensure static files are served before the SPA fallback for robots.txt, sitemap.xml, sitemap-products.xml, sitemap-products-*.xml, and merchant-products.xml.
Ensure production honors the _headers content-type rules or equivalent provider-level MIME rules.
Add non-secret Cloudflare zone/account/public URL metadata to the Mitra project wrapper and load API tokens only through Keychain-backed helpers before any provider write.

verification.command: /Users/chandler/.codex/skills/distill-heavy-output/scripts/distill_cmd.sh "Run the Mitra Auto production build for R-3. Return exactly: status passed/failed, generated output directory, whether static public assets were copied if reported, warnings grouped by tool/file, and fatal errors. Do not include routine Vite transform progress." -- npm run build

for url in http://127.0.0.1:4174/robots.txt http://127.0.0.1:4174/sitemap.xml http://127.0.0.1:4174/sitemap-products.xml http://127.0.0.1:4174/merchant-products.xml; do curl -sSIL -o /dev/null --max-time 20 -w '%{url_effective}|http=%{http_code}|content_type=%{content_type}|redirects=%{num_redirects}|size_download=%{size_download}\n' "$url"; done

nextTask: R-4 - HTTP redirect, product ID migration, and soft-404 remediation
